 843→    private fun ASTNode.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations(): ASTNode {
 844→        require(elementType == SECONDARY_CONSTRUCTOR || elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER)
 845→        return findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
 846→            ?.let { modifierList ->
 847→                modifierList
 848→                    .children()
 849→                    .firstOrNull { !it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.isPartOfComment() && it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
 850→                    ?: modifierList.nextCodeSibling()
 851→            }
 852→            ?: children().firstOrNull { !it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.isPartOfComment() }
 853→            ?: this
 854→    }
 855→
 856→    private fun ASTNode.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(): ASTNode {
 857→        var fromAstNode: ASTNode? = this
 858→        while (fromAstNode?.prevLeaf() != null &&
 859→            (fromAstNode.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpace() || fromAstNode.prevLeaf()?.isPartOfComment() == true)
 860→        ) {
 861→            fromAstNode = fromAstNode.prevLeaf()
 862→        }
 863→        return fromAstNode!!
 864→    }
 865→
 866→    private fun isPartOfBinaryExpressionWrappedInCondition(node: ASTNode) =
 867→        node
 868→            .parents()
 869→            .takeWhile { it.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION || it.elementType == CONDITION }
 870→            .lastOrNull()
 871→            ?.elementType == CONDITION
 872→
 873→    private fun currentIndent() =
 874→        indentContextStack
 875→            .peekLast()
 876→            .indent()
 877→
 878→    private fun startIndentContext(
 879→        fromAstNode: ASTNode,
 880→        toAstNode: ASTNode = fromAstNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 881→        nodeIndent: String = currentIndent(),
 882→        childIndent: String = indentConfig.indent,
 883→        firstChildIndent: String = childIndent,
 884→        lastChildIndent: String = childIndent,
 885→    ): IndentContext =
 886→        IndentContext(
 887→            fromASTNode = fromAstNode,
 888→            toASTNode = toAstNode,
 889→            nodeIndent = nodeIndent,
 890→            firstChildIndent = firstChildIndent,
 891→            childIndent = childIndent,
 892→            lastChildIndent = lastChildIndent,
 893→        ).also { newIndentContext ->
 894→            LOGGER.trace {
 895→                val nodeIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(newIndentContext.nodeIndent)
 896→                val childIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(newIndentContext.childIndent)
 897→                "Create new indent context (same as parent) with level ($nodeIndentLevel, $childIndentLevel) for " +
 898→                    "${fromAstNode.elementType}: ${newIndentContext.nodes}"
 899→            }
 900→            indentContextStack.addLast(newIndentContext)
 901→        }
 902→
 903→    override fun afterVisitChildNodes(
 904→        node: ASTNode,
 905→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 906→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 907→    ) {
 908→        while (indentContextStack.peekLast()?.toASTNode == node) {
 909→            LOGGER.trace {
 910→                val indentContext = indentContextStack.peekLast()
 911→                val nodeIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.nodeIndent)
 912→                val childIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.childIndent)
 913→                "Remove indent context with level ($nodeIndentLevel, $childIndentLevel) for ${indentContext.fromASTNode.elementType}: " +
 914→                    "${indentContext.nodes}"
 915→            }
 916→            indentContextStack
 917→                .removeLast()
 918→                .also {
 919→                    LOGGER.trace {
 920→                        val indentContext = indentContextStack.peekLast()
 921→                        val nodeIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.nodeIndent)
 922→                        val childIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.childIndent)
 923→                        "Last indent context with level ($nodeIndentLevel, $childIndentLevel) for " +
 924→                            "${indentContext.fromASTNode.elementType}: ${indentContext.nodes}"
 925→                    }
 926→                }
 927→        }
 928→    }
 929→
 930→    override fun afterLastNode() {
 931→        require(indentContextStack.isEmpty()) {
 932→            indentContextStack
 933→                .joinToString(prefix = "Stack should be empty:\n\t", separator = "\n\t") { it.toString() }
 934→        }
 935→    }
 936→
 937→    private fun visitNewLineIndentation(
 938→        node: ASTNode,
 939→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 940→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 941→    ) {
 942→        if (node.ignoreIndent()) {
 943→            return
 944→        }
 945→
 946→        val normalizedNodeIndent = node.normalizedIndent(emit)
 947→        val expectedIndentation = node.expectedIndent()
 948→        val text = node.text
 949→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
 950→        if (nodeIndent != normalizedNodeIndent || normalizedNodeIndent != expectedIndentation) {
 951→            if (normalizedNodeIndent != expectedIndentation) {
 952→                emit(
 953→                    node.startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
 954→                    "Unexpected indentation (${normalizedNodeIndent.length}) (should be ${expectedIndentation.length})",
 955→                    true,
 956→                )
 957→            } else {
 958→                // Indentation was at correct level but contained invalid indent characters. This violation has already
 959→                // been emitted.
 960→            }
 961→            LOGGER.trace {
 962→                "Line $line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "changed indentation to ${expectedIndentation.length} " +
 963→                    "(from ${normalizedNodeIndent.length}) for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}"
 964→            }
 965→            if (autoCorrect) {
 966→                (node as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
 967→                    text.substringBeforeLast("\n") + "\n" + expectedIndentation,
 968→                )
 969→            }
 970→        } else {
 971→            node.processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
 972→        }
 973→    }
 974→
 975→    private fun ASTNode.ignoreIndent(): Boolean {
 976→        val nextLeaf = nextLeaf()
 977→        if (text.endsWith("\n") && nextLeaf.isStartOfRawStringLiteral()) {
 978→            processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
 979→            return true // raw strings (""") are allowed at column 0
 980→        }
 981→
 982→        nextLeaf
 983→            ?.parent(strict = false) { it.psi is PsiComment }
 984→            ?.let { comment ->
 985→                if (text.endsWith("\n")) {
 986→                    processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
 987→                    return true // comments are allowed at column 0
 988→                }
 989→                if (comment.textContains('\n') && comment.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
 990→                    // FIXME: while we cannot assume any kind of layout inside a block comment,
 991→                    // `/*` and `*/` can still be indented
 992→                    processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
 993→                    return true
 994→                }
 995→            }
 996→        return false
 997→    }
 998→
 999→    private fun ASTNode?.isStartOfRawStringLiteral(): Boolean = this != null && this.elementType == OPEN_QUOTE && this.text == "\"\"\""
1000→

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.ELSE
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule.Mode.REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.column
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.indent
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isPartOfComment
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpace
 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline
 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 19→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 20→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 21→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiWhiteSpace
 22→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 23→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiWhiteSpaceImpl
 24→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtContainerNode
 25→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtDoWhileExpression
 26→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtIfExpression
 27→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtWhileExpression
 28→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.children
 29→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.getStrictParentOfType
 30→
 31→/**
 32→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#method-call-formatting
 33→ *
 34→ * The rule is more aggressive in inserting newlines after arguments than mentioned in the styleguide:
 35→ * Each argument should be on a separate line if
 36→ * - at least one of the arguments is
 37→ * - maxLineLength exceeded (and separating arguments with \n would actually help)
 38→ * in addition, "(" and ")" must be on separates line if any of the arguments are (otherwise on the same)
 39→ */
 40→public class ArgumentListWrappingRule :
 41→    StandardRule(
 42→        id = "argument-list-wrapping",
 43→        visitorModifiers =
 44→            setOf(
 45→                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 46→                    // ArgumentListWrapping should only be used in case after normal wrapping the max_line_length is still
 47→                    // violated
 48→                    ruleId = WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 49→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 50→                ),
 51→            ),
 52→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 53→            setOf(
 54→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 55→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 56→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 57→            ),
 58→    ) {
 59→    private var editorConfigIndent = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 60→
 61→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 62→
 63→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 64→        editorConfigIndent = IndentConfig(
 65→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 66→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 67→        )
 68→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 69→    }
 70→
 71→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 72→        node: ASTNode,
 73→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 74→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 75→    ) {
 76→        if (editorConfigIndent.disabled) {
 77→            return
 78→        }
 79→
 80→        if (node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST) {
 81→            if (needToWrapArgumentList(node)) {
 82→                val newIndentLevel = node.getNewIndentLevel()
 83→                node
 84→                    .children()
 85→                    .forEach { child -> wrapArgumentInList(newIndentLevel, child, emit, autoCorrect) }
 86→            }
 87→        }
 88→    }
 89→
 90→    private fun needToWrapArgumentList(node: ASTNode) =
 91→        if ( // skip when there are no arguments
 92→            node.firstChildNode?.treeNext?.elementType != ElementType.RPAR &&
 93→            // skip lambda arguments
 94→            node.treeParent?.elementType != ElementType.FUNCTION_LITERAL &&
 95→            // skip if number of arguments is big (we assume it with a magic number of 8)
 96→            node.children().count { it.elementType == ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT } <= 8
 97→        ) {
 98→            // each argument should be on a separate line if
 99→            // - at least one of the arguments is
100→            // - maxLineLength exceeded (and separating arguments with \n would actually help)
101→            // in addition, "(" and ")" must be on separates line if any of the arguments are (otherwise on the same)
102→            node.textContainsIgnoringLambda('\n') || node.exceedsMaxLineLength()
103→        } else {
104→            false
105→        }
106→
107→    private fun ASTNode.exceedsMaxLineLength() = (column - 1 + textLength) > maxLineLength && !textContains('\n')
108→
109→    private fun ASTNode.getNewIndentLevel(): Int {
110→        val currentIndentLevel = editorConfigIndent.indentLevelFrom(indent(false))
111→        return when {
112→            // IDEA quirk:
113→            // generic<
114→            //     T,
115→            //     R>(
116→            //     1,
117→            //     2
118→            // )
119→            // instead of
120→            // generic<
121→            //     T,
122→            //     R>(
123→            //         1,
124→            //         2
125→            //     )
126→            currentIndentLevel > 0 && hasTypeArgumentListInFront() -> currentIndentLevel - 1
127→
128→            // IDEA quirk:
129→            // foo
130→            //     .bar = Baz(
131→            //     1,
132→            //     2
133→            // )
134→            // instead of
135→            // foo
136→            //     .bar = Baz(
137→            //         1,
138→            //         2
139→            //     )
140→            currentIndentLevel > 0 && isPartOfDotQualifiedAssignmentExpression() -> currentIndentLevel - 1
141→
142→            else -> currentIndentLevel
143→        }.let {
144→            if (isOnSameLineAsControlFlowKeyword()) {
145→                it + 1
146→            } else {
147→                it
148→            }
149→        }
150→    }
151→
152→    private fun wrapArgumentInList(
153→        newIndentLevel: Int,
154→        child: ASTNode,
155→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
156→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
157→    ) {
158→        val indent = "\n" + editorConfigIndent.indent.repeat(newIndentLevel)
159→        when (child.elementType) {
160→            ElementType.LPAR -> {
161→                val prevLeaf = child.prevLeaf()
162→                if (prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace && prevLeaf.textContains('\n')) {
163→                    emit(child.startOffset, errorMessage(child), true)
164→                    if (autoCorrect) {
165→                        prevLeaf.delete()
166→                    }
167→                }
168→            }
169→            ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT,
170→            ElementType.RPAR,
171→            -> {
172→                // aiming for
173→                // ... LPAR
174→                // <line indent + indentSize> VALUE_PARAMETER...
175→                // <line indent> RPAR
176→                val intendedIndent =
177→                    if (child.elementType == ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT) {
178→                        indent + editorConfigIndent.indent
179→                    } else {
180→                        indent
181→                    }
182→                val prevLeaf = child.prevWhiteSpaceWithNewLine() ?: child.prevLeaf()
183→                if (prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace) {
184→                    if (prevLeaf.getText().contains("\n")) {
185→                        // The current child is already wrapped to a new line. Checking and fixing the
186→                        // correct size of the indent is the responsibility of the IndentationRule.
187→                        return
188→                    } else {
189→                        // The current child needs to be wrapped to a newline.
190→                        emit(child.startOffset, errorMessage(child), true)
191→                        if (autoCorrect) {
192→                            // The indentation is purely based on the previous leaf only. Note that in
193→                            // autoCorrect mode the indent rule, if enabled, runs after this rule and
194→                            // determines the final indentation. But if the indent rule is disabled then the
195→                            // indent of this rule is kept.
196→                            (prevLeaf as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(intendedIndent)
197→                        }
198→                    }
199→                } else {
200→                    // Insert a new whitespace element in order to wrap the current child to a new line.
201→                    emit(child.startOffset, errorMessage(child), true)
202→                    if (autoCorrect) {
203→                        child.treeParent.addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(intendedIndent), child)
204→                    }
205→                }
206→                // Indentation of child nodes need to be fixed by the IndentationRule.
207→            }
208→        }
209→    }
210→
211→    private fun errorMessage(node: ASTNode) =
212→        when (node.elementType) {
213→            ElementType.LPAR -> """Unnecessary newline before "(""""
214→            ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT ->
215→                "Argument should be on a separate line (unless all arguments can fit a single line)"
216→            ElementType.RPAR -> """Missing newline before ")""""
217→            else -> throw UnsupportedOperationException()
218→        }
219→
220→    private fun ASTNode.textContainsIgnoringLambda(char: Char): Boolean {
221→        return children().any { child ->
222→            val elementType = child.elementType
223→            elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE && child.textContains(char) ||
224→                elementType == ElementType.COLLECTION_LITERAL_EXPRESSION && child.textContains(char) ||
225→                elementType == ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT && child.children().any { it.textContainsIgnoringLambda(char) }
226→        }
227→    }
228→
229→    private fun ASTNode.hasTypeArgumentListInFront(): Boolean =
230→        treeParent.children()
231→            .firstOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST }
232→            ?.children()
233→            ?.any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() } == true
234→
235→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfDotQualifiedAssignmentExpression(): Boolean =
236→        treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == ElementType.BINARY_EXPRESSION &&
237→            treeParent?.treeParent?.children()?.find { it.elementType == ElementType.DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION } != null
238→
239→    private fun ASTNode.prevWhiteSpaceWithNewLine(): ASTNode? {
240→        var prev = prevLeaf()
241→        while (prev != null && (prev.isWhiteSpace() || prev.isPartOfComment())) {
242→            if (prev.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
243→                return prev
244→            }
245→            prev = prev.prevLeaf()
246→        }
247→        return null
248→    }
249→
250→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsControlFlowKeyword(): Boolean {
251→        val containerNode = psi.getStrictParentOfType<KtContainerNode>() ?: return false
252→        if (containerNode.node.elementType == ELSE) return false
253→        val controlFlowKeyword =
254→            when (val parent = containerNode.parent) {
255→                is KtIfExpression -> parent.ifKeyword.node
256→                is KtWhileExpression -> parent.firstChild.node
257→                is KtDoWhileExpression -> parent.whileKeyword?.node
258→                else -> null
259→            } ?: return false
260→
261→        var prevLeaf = prevLeaf() ?: return false
262→        while (prevLeaf != controlFlowKeyword) {
263→            if (prevLeaf.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) return false
264→            prevLeaf = prevLeaf.prevLeaf() ?: return false
265→        }
266→        return true
267→    }
268→}
269→
270→public val ARGUMENT_LIST_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = ArgumentListWrappingRule().ruleId
271→

 76→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 77→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.tree.IElementType
 78→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.tree.TokenSet

 84→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 85→
 86→/**
 87→ * This rule inserts missing newlines (e.g. between parentheses of a multi-line function call). This logic previously
 88→ * was part of the IndentationRule (phase 1).
 89→ *
 90→ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 91→ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 92→ * indentation rule is not run.
 93→ */
 94→public class WrappingRule :
 95→    StandardRule(
 96→        id = "wrapping",
 97→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 98→            setOf(
 99→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
100→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
101→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
102→            ),
103→        visitorModifiers =
104→            setOf(
105→                RunAfterRule(
106→                    ruleId = ANNOTATION_RULE_ID,
107→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
108→                ),
109→            ),
110→    ) {
111→    private var line = 1
112→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
113→    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
114→
115→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
116→        line = 1
117→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
118→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
119→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
120→        )
121→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
122→    }
123→
124→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
125→        node: ASTNode,
126→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
127→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
128→    ) {
129→        when (node.elementType) {
130→            BLOCK -> beforeVisitBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
131→            LPAR, LBRACKET -> rearrangeBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit) // TODO: LT
132→            SUPER_TYPE_LIST -> rearrangeSuperTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
133→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST, VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> rearrangeValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
134→            TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST, TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> rearrangeTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
135→//            TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> rearrangeTypeParameterList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
136→            ARROW -> rearrangeArrow(node, autoCorrect, emit)
137→            WHITE_SPACE -> line += node.text.count { it == '\n' }
138→            CLOSING_QUOTE -> rearrangeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
139→        }
140→    }
141→
142→    private fun beforeVisitBlock(
143→        node: ASTNode,
144→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
145→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
146→    ) {
147→        require(node.elementType == BLOCK)
148→
149→        val startOfBlock = node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
150→        if (startOfBlock?.elementType != LBRACE) {
151→            return
152→        }
153→        val blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline = startOfBlock.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
154→        val endOfBlock = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
155→        val blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline = endOfBlock?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
156→        val wrapBlock =
157→            when {
158→                startOfBlock.isPartOf(LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) -> {
159→                    // String template inside raw string literal may exceed the maximum line length
160→                    false
161→                }
162→                blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline -> false
163→                node.textContains('\n') || blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline -> {
164→                    // A multiline block should always be wrapped unless it starts with an EOL comment
165→                    node.firstChildLeafOrSelf().elementType != EOL_COMMENT
166→                }
167→                maxLineLength != MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF -> {
168→                    val lengthUntilBeginOfLine =
169→                        node
170→                            .leaves(false)
171→                            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
172→                            .sumOf { it.textLength }
173→                    val lengthUntilEndOfLine =
174→                        node
175→                            .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
176→                            .leavesIncludingSelf()
177→                            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
178→                            .sumOf { it.textLength }
179→                    lengthUntilBeginOfLine + lengthUntilEndOfLine > maxLineLength
180→                }
181→                else -> false
182→            }
183→        if (wrapBlock) {
184→            startOfBlock
185→                .takeIf { !it.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
186→                ?.let { leafNodeBeforeBlock ->
187→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
188→                        leafNodeBeforeBlock,
189→                        autoCorrect,
190→                        emit,
191→                    )
192→                }
193→        }
194→    }
195→
196→    private fun rearrangeBlock(
197→        node: ASTNode,
198→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
199→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
200→    ) {
201→        val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[node.elementType]
202→        var newlineInBetween = false
203→        var parameterListInBetween = false
204→        var numberOfArgs = 0
205→        var firstArg: ASTNode? = null
206→        // matching ), ] or }
207→        val r = node.nextSibling {
208→            val isValueArgument = it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT
209→            val hasLineBreak = if (isValueArgument) it.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN) else it.hasLineBreak()
210→            newlineInBetween = newlineInBetween || hasLineBreak
211→            parameterListInBetween = parameterListInBetween || it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST
212→            if (isValueArgument) {
213→                numberOfArgs++
214→                firstArg = it
215→            }
216→            it.elementType == rElementType
217→        }!!
218→        if (
219→            !newlineInBetween ||
220→            // keep { p ->
221→            // }
222→            (node.elementType == LBRACE && parameterListInBetween) ||
223→            // keep ({
224→            // }) and (object : C {
225→            // })
226→            (
227→                numberOfArgs == 1 &&
228→                    firstArg?.firstChildNode?.elementType
229→                        ?.let { it == OBJECT_LITERAL || it == LAMBDA_EXPRESSION } == true
230→                )
231→        ) {
232→            return
233→        }
234→        if (node.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()) {
235→            // keep:
236→            // for (foo in listOf(
237→            //     "foo-1",
238→            //     "foo-2"
239→            // )) { ... }
240→            // but reject:
241→            // for (
242→            //     foo in listOf(
243→            //         "foo-1",
244→            //         "foo-2"
245→            //     )
246→            // ) { ... }
247→            return
248→        }
249→        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf {
250→                // Skip comments, whitespace, and empty nodes
251→                !it.isPartOfComment() &&
252→                    !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() &&
253→                    it.textLength > 0
254→            }.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
255→            // IDEA quirk:
256→            // if (true &&
257→            //     true
258→            // ) {
259→            // }
260→            // instead of
261→            // if (
262→            //     true &&
263→            //     true
264→            // ) {
265→            // }
266→            node.treeNext?.elementType != CONDITION
267→        ) {
268→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
269→        }
270→        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
271→            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.treeParent.indent())
272→        }
273→    }
274→
275→    private fun rearrangeSuperTypeList(
276→        node: ASTNode,
277→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
278→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
279→    ) {
280→        val entries = (node.psi as KtSuperTypeList).entries
281→        if (
282→            node.textContains('\n') &&
283→            entries.size > 1 &&
284→            // e.g.
285→            //
286→            // class A : B, C,
287→            //     D
288→            // or
289→            // class A : B, C({
290→            // }), D
291→            //
292→            // but not
293→            //
294→            // class A : B, C, D({
295→            // })
296→            !(
297→                entries.dropLast(1).all { it.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY } &&
298→                    entries.last().elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY
299→                )
300→        ) {
301→            // put space after :
302→            if (!node.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
303→                val colon = node.prevCodeLeaf()!!
304→                if (
305→                    !colon.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
306→                    colon.prevCodeLeaf().let { it?.elementType != RPAR || !it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
307→                ) {
308→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit)
309→                }
310→            }
311→            // put entries on separate lines
312→            // TODO: group emit()s below with the one above into one (similar to ParameterListWrappingRule)
313→            for (c in node.children()) {
314→                if (c.elementType == COMMA &&
315→                    !c.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
316→                    !c.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine()
317→                ) {
318→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
319→                        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired = c,
320→                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
321→                        emit = emit,
322→                        indent = node.indent(),
323→                    )
324→                }
325→            }
326→        }
327→    }
328→
329→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine() =
330→        nextLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
331→            ?.isPartOfComment() == true
332→
333→    private fun rearrangeValueList(
334→        node: ASTNode,
335→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
336→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
337→    ) {
338→        for (c in node.children()) {
339→            val hasLineBreak =
340→                when (c.elementType) {
341→                    VALUE_ARGUMENT -> c.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN)
342→                    VALUE_PARAMETER, ANNOTATION -> c.hasLineBreak()
343→                    else -> false
344→                }
345→            if (hasLineBreak) {
346→                // rearrange
347→                //
348→                // a, b, value(
349→                // ), c, d
350→                //
351→                // to
352→                //
353→                // a, b,
354→                // value(
355→                // ),
356→                // c, d
357→
358→                // insert \n in front of multi-line value
359→                val prevSibling = c.prevSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
360→                if (
361→                    prevSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
362→                    !prevSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
363→                ) {
364→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(prevSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
365→                }
366→                // insert \n after multi-line value
367→                val nextSibling = c.nextSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
368→                val hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter =
369→                    c.isLastValueParameter() && c.firstChildNode.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION
370→                if (
371→                    nextSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
372→                    !hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter &&
373→                    !nextSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
374→                    // value(
375→                    // ), // a comment
376→                    // c, d
377→                    nextSibling.treeNext?.treeNext?.psi !is PsiComment
378→                ) {
379→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(nextSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
380→                }
381→            }
382→        }
383→    }
384→
385→    private fun ASTNode.isLastValueParameter() =
386→        elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER &&
387→            siblings().none { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
388→
389→    private fun rearrangeTypeArgumentList(
390→        node: ASTNode,
391→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
392→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
393→    ) {
394→        if (node.textContains('\n')) {
395→            // Each type projection must be preceded with a whitespace containing a newline
396→            node
397→                .children()
398→                .filter { it.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION || it.elementType == TYPE_PARAMETER }
399→                .forEach { typeProjection ->
400→                    typeProjection
401→                        .prevSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
402→                        .let { prevSibling ->
403→                            if (prevSibling?.elementType == LT || prevSibling.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline()) {
404→                                emit(typeProjection.startOffset, "A newline was expected before '${typeProjection.text}'", true)
405→                                if (autoCorrect) {
406→                                    typeProjection.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
407→                                        node.treeParent.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent),
408→                                    )
409→                                }
410→                            }
411→                        }
412→                }
413→
414→            // After the last type projection a whitespace containing a newline must exist
415→            node
416→                .findChildByType(GT)
417→                ?.let { closingAngle ->
418→                    val prevSibling = closingAngle.prevSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
419→                    if (prevSibling?.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || prevSibling.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline()) {
420→                        emit(closingAngle.startOffset, "A newline was expected before '${closingAngle.text}'", true)
421→                        if (autoCorrect) {
422→                            closingAngle.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
423→                                node.treeParent.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent),
424→                            )
425→                        }
426→                    }
427→                }
428→
429→            Unit
430→        }
431→    }
432→
433→    private fun rearrangeClosingQuote(
434→        node: ASTNode,
435→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
436→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
437→    ) {
438→        node
439→            .treeParent
440→            .takeIf { it.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE }
441→            ?.let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
442→            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
443→            ?.takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
444→            ?.takeIf { node.treePrev.text.isNotBlank() }
445→            ?.let {
446→                // rewriting
447→                // """
448→                //     text
449→                // _""".trimIndent()
450→                // to
451→                // """
452→                //     text
453→                // _
454→                // """.trimIndent()
455→                emit(
456→                    node.startOffset,
457→                    "Missing newline before \"\"\"",
458→                    true,
459→                )
460→                if (autoCorrect) {
461→                    node as LeafPsiElement
462→                    node.rawInsertBeforeMe(LeafPsiElement(LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY, "\n"))
463→                }
464→                LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before (closing) \"\"\"" }
465→            }
466→    }
467→
468→    private fun mustBeFollowedByNewline(node: ASTNode): Boolean {
469→        // find EOL token (last token before \n)
470→        // if token is in lTokenSet
471→        //     find matching rToken
472→        //     return true if there is no newline after the rToken
473→        // return false
474→        val nextCodeSibling = node.nextCodeSibling() // e.g. BINARY_EXPRESSION
475→        var lToken = nextCodeSibling?.nextLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }?.prevCodeLeaf()
476→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType !in LTOKEN_SET) {
477→            // special cases:
478→            // x = y.f({ z ->
479→            // })
480→            // x = y.f(0, 1,
481→            // 2, 3)
482→            lToken = lToken.prevLeaf { it.elementType in LTOKEN_SET || it == node }
483→        }
484→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType in LTOKEN_SET) {
485→            val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[lToken.elementType]
486→            val rToken = lToken.nextSibling { it.elementType == rElementType }
487→            return rToken?.treeParent == lToken.treeParent
488→        }
489→        if (nextCodeSibling?.textContains('\n') == false) {
490→            return true
491→        }
492→        return false
493→    }
494→
495→    private fun rearrangeArrow(
496→        node: ASTNode,
497→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
498→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
499→    ) {
500→        val p = node.treeParent
501→        if (
502→            // check
503→            // `{ p -> ... }`
504→            // and
505→            // `when { m -> ... }`
506→            // only
507→            p.elementType.let { it != FUNCTION_LITERAL && it != WHEN_ENTRY } ||
508→            // ... and only if expression after -> spans multiple lines
509→            !p.textContains('\n') ||
510→            // permit
511→            // when {
512→            //     m -> 0 + d({
513→            //     })
514→            // }
515→            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && mustBeFollowedByNewline(node)) ||
516→            // permit
517→            // when (this) {
518→            //     in 0x1F600..0x1F64F, // Emoticons
519→            //     0x200D // Zero-width Joiner
520→            //     -> true
521→            // }
522→            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && node.prevLeaf()?.textContains('\n') == true)
523→        ) {
524→            return
525→        }
526→        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
527→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
528→        }
529→        val r = node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE } ?: return
530→        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
531→            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.indent())
532→        }
533→    }
534→
535→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
536→        node: ASTNode,
537→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
538→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
539→        indent: String,
540→    ) {
541→        emit(
542→            node.startOffset - 1,
543→            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
544→            true,
545→        )
546→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
547→        if (autoCorrect) {
548→            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indent)
549→        }
550→    }
551→
552→    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
553→        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
554→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
555→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
556→        indent: String? = null,
557→        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
558→    ) {
559→        emit(
560→            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
561→            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
562→            true,
563→        )
564→        LOGGER.trace {
565→            "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"
566→        }
567→        if (autoCorrect) {
568→            val tempIndent = indent ?: (nodeToFix.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
569→            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(tempIndent)
570→        }
571→    }
572→
573→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isMultiLine(): Boolean {
574→        for (child in node.children()) {
575→            if (child.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) {
576→                val v = child.text
577→                if (v == "\n") {
578→                    return true
579→                }
580→            }
581→        }
582→        return false
583→    }
584→
585→    private fun ASTNode.hasLineBreak(vararg ignoreElementTypes: IElementType): Boolean {
586→        if (isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) return true
587→        return if (ignoreElementTypes.isEmpty()) {
588→            textContains('\n')
589→        } else {
590→            elementType !in ignoreElementTypes &&
591→                children().any { c -> c.textContains('\n') && c.elementType !in ignoreElementTypes }
592→        }
593→    }
594→
595→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimIndent() = isFollowedBy("trimIndent()")
596→
597→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimMargin() = isFollowedBy("trimMargin()")
598→
599→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedBy(callExpressionName: String) =
600→        this.node.nextSibling { it.elementType != DOT }
601→            .let { it?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION && it.text == callExpressionName }
602→
603→    /**
604→     *  Allow for-statement in which only the expression contains a newline:
605→     *     for (foo in listOf(
606→     *         "foo-1",
607→     *         "foo-2"
608→     *     )) { ... }
609→     * but reject:
610→     *     for (
611→     *         foo in listOf(
612→     *             "foo-1",
613→     *             "foo-2"
614→     *         )
615→     *     ) { ... }
616→     */
617→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression(): Boolean {
618→        if (treeParent.elementType != ElementType.FOR) {
619→            return false
620→        }
621→        if (this.elementType != LPAR) {
622→            return treeParent.findChildByType(LPAR)!!.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()
623→        }
624→        require(elementType == LPAR) {
625→            "Node should be the LPAR of the FOR loop"
626→        }
627→
628→        var node: ASTNode? = this
629→        while (node != null && node.elementType != RPAR) {
630→            if (node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
631→                return false
632→            }
633→            node = node.nextSibling()
634→        }
635→        return true
636→    }
637→
638→    override fun afterVisitChildNodes(
639→        node: ASTNode,
640→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
641→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
642→    ) {
643→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK) {
644→            val startOfBlock = node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
645→            if (startOfBlock?.elementType != LBRACE) {
646→                return
647→            }
648→            val blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline = startOfBlock.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
649→            val endOfBlock = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
650→            val blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline = endOfBlock?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
651→            val wrapBlock =
652→                !blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline && (
653→                    blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline || node.textContains('\n')
654→                    )
655→            if (wrapBlock && endOfBlock != null) {
656→                requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
657→                    endOfBlock,
658→                    autoCorrect,
659→                    emit,
660→                    node.treeParent.indent(),
661→                )
662→            }
663→        }
664→    }
665→
666→    private companion object {
667→        private val LTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(LPAR, LBRACE, LBRACKET, LT)
668→        private val RTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(RPAR, RBRACE, RBRACKET, GT)
669→        private val MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP =
670→            LTOKEN_SET.types.zip(
671→                RTOKEN_SET.types,
672→            ).toMap()
673→    }
674→}
675→
676→public val WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = WrappingRule().ruleId

 46→/**
 47→ * This rule wraps each multiline expression to a newline.
 48→ */
 49→public class MultilineExpressionWrapping :
 50→    StandardRule(
 51→        id = "multiline-expression-wrapping",
 52→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 53→            setOf(
 54→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 55→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 56→            ),
 57→    ),
 58→    Rule.Experimental,
 59→    Rule.OfficialCodeStyle {
 60→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 61→
 62→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 63→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 64→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 65→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 66→        )
 67→    }
 68→
 69→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 70→        node: ASTNode,
 71→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 72→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 73→    ) {
 74→        if (node.elementType in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION &&
 75→            node.treeParent.elementType !in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION
 76→        ) {
 77→            visitExpression(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 78→        }
 79→        if (node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION &&
 80→            node.treeParent.elementType != BINARY_EXPRESSION
 81→        ) {
 82→            visitExpression(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 83→        }
 84→    }
 85→
 86→    private fun visitExpression(
 87→        node: ASTNode,
 88→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 89→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 90→    ) {
 91→        if (node.containsWhitespaceWithNewline() && node.needToWrapMultilineExpression()) {
 92→            node
 93→                .prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() }
 94→                .let { prevLeaf ->
 95→                    if (prevLeaf != null && !prevLeaf.textContains('\n')) {
 96→                        emit(node.startOffset, "A multiline expression should start on a new line", true)
 97→                        if (autoCorrect) {
 98→                            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
 99→                                node
100→                                    .treeParent
101→                                    .indent()
102→                                    .plus(indentConfig.indent),
103→                            )
104→                            node
105→                                .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
106→                                .nextLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpace() && it.elementType != COMMA }
107→                                ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(node.treeParent.indent())
108→                        }
109→                    }
110→                }
111→        }
112→    }
113→
114→    private fun ASTNode.containsWhitespaceWithNewline(): Boolean {
115→        val lastLeaf = lastChildLeafOrSelf()
116→        return firstChildLeafOrSelf()
117→            .leavesIncludingSelf()
118→            .takeWhile { it != lastLeaf }
119→            .any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() || it.isRegularStringPartWithNewline() }
120→    }
121→
122→    private fun ASTNode.isRegularStringPartWithNewline() =
123→        elementType == ElementType.REGULAR_STRING_PART &&
124→            text.startsWith("\n")
125→
126→    private fun ASTNode.needToWrapMultilineExpression() =
127→        isValueInAnAssignment() ||
128→            isLambdaExpression() ||
129→            isValueArgument() ||
130→            isAfterArrow()
131→
132→    private fun ASTNode.isValueInAnAssignment() =
133→        null !=
134→            prevCodeSibling()
135→                ?.takeIf { it.elementType == EQ }
136→                ?.takeUnless {
137→                    it.closingParenthesisOfFunctionOrNull()
138→                        ?.prevLeaf()
139→                        .isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
140→                }
141→
142→    private fun ASTNode.closingParenthesisOfFunctionOrNull() =
143→        takeIf { treeParent.elementType == FUN }
144→            ?.prevCodeLeaf()
145→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == RPAR }
146→
147→    private fun ASTNode.isLambdaExpression() =
148→        null !=
149→            treeParent
150→                .takeIf {
151→                    // Function literals in lambda expression have an implicit block (no LBRACE and RBRACE). So only wrap when the node is
152→                    // the first node in the block
153→                    it.elementType == BLOCK && it.firstChildNode == this
154→                }?.treeParent
155→                ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.FUNCTION_LITERAL }
156→                ?.treeParent
157→                ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.LAMBDA_EXPRESSION }
158→
159→    private fun ASTNode.isValueArgument() = treeParent.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT
160→
161→    private fun ASTNode.isAfterArrow() = prevCodeLeaf()?.elementType == ARROW
162→
163→    private companion object {
164→        // Based  on https://kotlinlang.org/spec/expressions.html#expressions
165→        val CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION =
166→            setOf(
167→                ARRAY_ACCESS_EXPRESSION,
168→                BINARY_WITH_TYPE,
169→                CALL_EXPRESSION,
170→                DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION,
171→                IF,
172→                IS_EXPRESSION,
173→                OBJECT_LITERAL,
174→                PREFIX_EXPRESSION,
175→                REFERENCE_EXPRESSION,
176→                SAFE_ACCESS_EXPRESSION,
177→                TRY,
178→                WHEN,
179→            )
180→    }
181→}
182→
183→public val MULTILINE_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = MultilineExpressionWrapping().ruleId
184→
